info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Schedule Appointments API
info.description: The Appointment Scheduling API enables businesses to efficiently manage and streamline their appointment-setting process. This API allows users to create, update, and manage appointments for services, ensuring a seamless integration within scheduling applications. Common consumers include service providers, healthcare systems, and appointment management platforms that require robust scheduling capabilities. Enhance your operational efficiency and customer experience by utilizing this API for managing your appointment workflows.

post.description: This endpoint allows users to create a new appointment in the system. The request must include all necessary details such as date, time, and participant information to successfully schedule the appointment.

requestBody.description: Request body for creating a new appointment. It must contain all the necessary information required for scheduling an appointment.

200.description: Request completed successfully. The appointment details are returned.

502.description: Bad gateway: The server received an invalid response from the upstream server.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

404.description: The requested appointment resource could not be found.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Error Response for Appointment Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the status of the operation is successful.

error.description: Short description of the error type.

message.description: A detailed message describing the error.
message.example: Unable to process bank account creation request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The API path where the error occurred.

trace.description: A trace of the error for debugging purposes.

suggestion.description: Suggested actions to resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the structure of an error response for failed appointment operations.

example.message: Unable to process bank account creation request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database timeout occurred while saving account information.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.title: Error Details

customer.description: Indicates whether the error pertains to a customer.

errorCode.description: A specific code representing the error type.

message.description: A specific message associated with the error details.
message.example: The specified appointment does not exist.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.description: This schema provides additional details on the error that occurred.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Appointment Response

policyId.description: The unique identifier for the insurance policy.

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer associated with the policy.

policyType.description: The type of insurance policy (e.g., health, auto, life).

coverageAmount.description: The total coverage amount of the policy.

premiumAmount.description: The amount that needs to be paid as premium for the policy.

status.description: The current status of the policy (e.g., active, inactive, pending).

startDate.description: The start date of the policy coverage.

endDate.description: The end date of the policy coverage.

message.description: Additional messages related to the policy creation.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Response schema for the appointment creation operation, containing details about the created policy.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: 502 Error Response for Appointment Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the operation was successful or not.

error.description: A brief description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message about the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for policy creation.

path.description: The API path where the error occurred.

trace.description: A trace ID or additional context for debugging.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for how to resolve the issue.
suggestion.example: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the response structure for a 502 error during appointment operations. It includes details about the error, suggestions for resolution, and associated timestamp.

example.message: Customer details not found for policy creation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Error Response for Appointments Operation

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: A flag indicating the status of the operation, usually false for errors.

error.description: A brief description of the error type.

message.description: A more detailed message about the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for policy creation.

path.description: The path of the request that caused the error.

trace.description: A trace of the error for debugging purposes.

details.description: Additional details regarding the error that occurred.

suggestion.description: Helpful suggestions for the user on how to resolve the issue.
suggestion.example: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the error response returned when a POST operation on appointments fails with a 500 status code.

example.message: Customer details not found for policy creation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Appointment Request Body

customernumber.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

coverageAmount.description: The total coverage amount for the policy.

premiumAmount.description: The premium amount to be paid for the policy.

startDate.description: The start date of the policy coverage, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

endDate.description: The end date of the policy coverage, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

beneficiarycode.description: The identifier for the beneficiary of the policy.

paymentmode.description: The mode of payment for the premium.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the request body for creating an appointment.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Appointment Error Response

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the request was successful.

error.description: A short description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message explaining the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for policy creation.

path.description: The path of the request that resulted in the error.

trace.description: A stack trace or additional debugging information related to the error.

suggestion.description: Suggestions or solutions for resolving the error.
suggestion.example: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the error response returned when an appointment operation fails with a 404 status code.

example.message: Customer details not found for policy creation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates which headers were used to determine the response variant.

Location.description: Provides the URL to the newly created or retrieved resource.

x-mock-200.description: Used to indicate a mock response status, useful in testing scenarios where a real server response is not available.
